hero.tagline: I design products people use — then I build them.
hero.description: **4 years** of full-cycle product work — research, Figma, production code. I bridge both sides: what users need and what the business must achieve. **10+ live launches** across SaaS, fintech, and web — **10+ international clients**.

about.title: Design built to survive the handoff
about.body: Most handoffs lose something between Figma and code. I close that gap myself — I design in Figma and can read/build in React, Vue, and Tailwind, so what I design is what actually ships, pixel for pixel.
about.designerBody: Research first, then wireframes, systems, and high-fidelity screens that hold up under real use.
about.engineerBody: Fluent enough in React, Vue, and Tailwind to hand off cleanly — or ship it myself, pixel for pixel.

aiTools.title: AI is built into how I work.
aiTools.body: From research to production, AI tooling helps me iterate faster and explore more directions — so more of my time goes to the decisions that actually matter.
aiTools.claudeCodeBody: My everyday design partner — connected to Figma through the Figma MCP, with custom skills that speed up real UI/UX work without giving up control of the details.
aiTools.claudeDesignBody: An AI design-generation tool I use to spin up layouts and visual directions fast — great for gathering references and exploring ideas before refining in Figma.
aiTools.aiStudioBody: Building real, working products end-to-end — like FanLearn, which I prototyped during a vibe-coding challenge.

experience.title: Four years, hands-on the whole way.
experience.lead: A track record of designing and shipping real products — backed by a proven freelance record.

process.title: From problem to shipped — I stay across the whole path.
process.step1Body: Start with why: the user's problem and the business outcome, so design decisions have a reason.
process.step2Body: Wireframe, build the system, and prototype in Figma until the flow earns its place.
process.step3Body: Take it to production myself in clean, responsive, accessible code — so what ships matches the design.
process.step4Body: Use AI across the process to compress timelines while keeping the bar high.

work.title: Work that starts with a problem.
work.lead: How I approach each project — the problem, the method, the business behind it, and the outcome.

project.protectedBody: The final visuals are protected. I can share sanitized, non-final work that shows the thinking and process — just reach out.

testimonials.title: What people say about working with me.

contact.title: Let's build something worth shipping.
contact.body: I'm open to new projects and roles. Share a few lines about what you need — I usually reply within a day.
contact.success: Thanks — your message is on its way. I'll reply soon.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again or email me directly.

footer.tagline: UI/UX Designer who designs products people use — and builds them too.

resume.title: Download Resume
resume.subtitle: Available in two languages — pick the one you need.
